reviews.review_text:
Police Simulator is actually a well-made game if you’re looking for a realistic look at day-to-day law enforcement. It focuses on the details that most cop games skip — things like proper procedures, writing tickets, handling accidents, and dealing with situations step-by-step instead of just chasing and arresting everyone. I appreciate that it lets you actually do the job rather than turning everything into a shootout.

I will say, im 5 hours in currently and still haven't seen much action. First few shifts are repetetive with parking tickets, jaywalking, littering. Then you get into on scene accidents with car crashes, traffic stops where you can search for drugs. I do like being thurough with everything. A lot of bugs for a game thats been out for awhile, and it seems like theyre focusing on DLC's to make more money, rather than fixing their already released content. Still feels like it's in early access.

TAKE THIS AS A WARNING, THIS GAME IS BROKEN, YOU ESSENTIALLY CAN NOT PLAY MULTIPLAYER ANYMORE AND THE DEVS HAVE ESSENTIALLY ABANDONED IT. THIS GAME IS 100% NOT WORTH THE PRICE. AND DONT PURCHASE AND EXPECT TO PLAY WITH YOUR FRIENDS

Police Patrol Officers is a really cool game with fun gameplay and great police action. I enjoy playing it a lot, especially driving the different police cars and patrolling around the city. One thing that would make the game even better is adding multiplayer support for more players. Right now, you can spawn up to 4 police cars, but only 2 players can play together. It would be awesome if the multiplayer limit could be increased to 4 players so friends can all play together at the same time. Overall, it’s a fun and enjoyable game with a lot of potential!

Bought it when it was 65% discount. Definitely worth the money for me who's playing game after a stressful 12h shift at work. Yeah i found bugs here and there but it doesnt not really matter at this point since it was not a competitive game. Totally recommended for those who wants to kill time before bed time waiting for your brain to get sleepy

The core gameplay loop is promising BUT:
Game is as or even more buggy than what could be expected from a game that just released into early access but apparently this game is "finished". 
1. Controls just stop working randomly forcing you to restart the game.
2. Game routinely crashes.
3. Audio and graphical glitches.
4. AI that goes completely insane.
5. Multiplayer that's barely working.
6. And a long list of other issues.

2 people can play together at the maximum. Don't really know why but since the multiplayer experience is held together by tape maybe it's for the best. During my latest 6 hours of gameplay, my friend and I have had at least 20 separate crashes each and constant disconnects. Trying to join a session will result in a "fatal error" 75% of the time. 

And besides the technical issues, there's a lack of QoL-features such as being able to adjust your gameplay experience in more detail. I would sell my fridge just in order to be able to not have to sit through every identical animation of handing someone their ID back.

In summary, trying to play this game is just like being at the casino. You still try to play but the cards are always stacked against you and more times than not, you'll be on the losing side. Really disappointing that they managed to squeeze out a bunch of barebones DLC but couldn't manage to make a game that functions better than a random asset flip you'd find when browsing the internet at 4am, heavily sleep deprived, drunk and possibly having a stroke. 

Would not recommend this game solely on the fact that this shouldn't in any case ever be allowed to be defined as a "finished" game.

I bought this game years ago with the hopes that the devs would really commit to developing and improving it.  Unfortunately that never happened, and instead of updating and fixing the base game, they just made all their updates minor content additions and are paid DLCs.  At this point, it's not worth it unless a sale brings it below $5.

I think its ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ crazy to charge for DLC in this game. To have anything outside of the base game uniform, car, and call outs, you have to pay for everything. That is kind of wild for me personally. All this game had or could of had going for it was unlocking better stuff as you patrolled. Its sad you have it all locked behind pay walls... Hope yall company goes bankrupt. You deserve it after slapping all of us in the face with this ♥♥♥♥. I bought in early access too. Scum Bags
surprising fun sim game! u got to play as a patrol officer and go through the whole procedure of all sort of cases. strongly recommend to get during a discount!

It's a fun game, but there are a LOT of bugs and the developers don't seem to be actively maintaining the game. Hopefully someone else releases a game along these lines because it's fun, but needs an active developer to keep it alive. This game appears abandoned.

currently the best police simulator ever. they should maybe add that some of the npcs can shoot at you if they have guns and you'll have the chance to shoot at them and make more buildings interactable like kiosks, hotels, resturants, and etc. then add emergency call of armed robbery that robbers are trying to rob places like those interactable building with guns that they will shoot the police. would be W and fun as hell. I'm really expecting updates like that!

It's quite repetitive and requires a bit of a grind to unlock the fun stuff. It focuses on realism instead of action. If you have the patience and are looking for something chill and realistic, then give this a go. If not then you'll probably be disappointed.
Really fun, a little buggy but worth the money I spent on it. Also a pretty easy 100% if that interests you

There are aspects about this game that needs to be expanded on, but also the progression once you've completed basically everything. There's no progression up the ranks. Even with the extra DLCs you only get the abilities to do those missions.

This is the definition of unreal engine hell. The shadow always were messed up, the city felt so empty, it feels like a game where you would at least spend £10 on for the base game not £22.49. It feels as if the developers just slapped it together during their lunch break as all the assist especially the building and textures feel so cheap. Also if you run into a car it goes through you? So you are now a ghost police?  Hopefully the devs fix this game.

Crashes on windows 11!

Find game in list in library
Right click game title
In general, find launch options
Type -dx11
This uses direct x 11 for more stability
Launch game, no need to restart steam

Otherwise, fun game!  I enjoy harassing npc's

The gameplay and graphics are mostly fine, but the fact that the developers have not updated the game in nearly 5 months (at the time of this view) is insane, the game is riddled with bugs and multiplayer is nearly unplayable.
very fun game, realistic gameplay and lots to do.

It is a good game, but it has a lot of bugs from funny to annoying. It Just genuinely needs to be fixed. Also, I want more voice lines for the game. It’s very little and you keep hearing the same thing over and over the cosmetics are very short. I wish there was more it’s a good game just needs a lot of work and I hope the devs can do that.
Ive played game for 20 minutes. I was stuck in the menu. And the game raped me to death. I recomand it if youve ever wanted to feel raped.

This game is great and is awesome if you are looking for a realistic game! But if you wanna have fun this game is probably not for you. I hope this game gets better as it develops as it does have some bugs but they don't effect your gameplay.

Great game if you only wanted to play single player, once you or a host starts up a mulltiplayer match the friends who join, lag and rubberband all over the shop. Also found the every interaction is delayed by a good 3-4 seconds, my setup should allow this game to run smoothly, great designed game. Poor effort for making multiplayer streamline.
has it's bugs but still fun enough for causal mindless play. idk if i'd waste my money on any dlc since the devs have pretty much abandoned it but if it's on sale i'd say grab it.
Great simluator. Love it. NPCs are somtimes annoying but it's definetly the best police simulator out there!

I like this game. So in the game you're a officer and when you start, you get no car and you have to do shifts on foot like interact with people if they litter, jaywalk, or anything that gives them a vilation. You can also give out parking tikets to car that parked in the wrong spots. There is SP which you earn, and CP where it takes points off if you do something dumb or wrong (i never lose cp). When you safe enough money and buy a car, you respond to calls by dispatch like accidents and idk other. Also there is a second area which is highway shifts which is a dlc. With the dlc you respond to calls like set up roadblocks, clear debris, transport arrestees, look for wanted suspects, reckless drivers, drug dealing, noise complaints, traffic stops, and vangrancy reports (idk what vangrancy is).

This game is a lot of fun and has a lot of potential. However it does seem that the DEVS have given up on the game for further updates to the game. It's excellent though for those who love to RP with  others!
My game crashed and all my progress has been reset to the beginning. I have to do everything again like wtf is this autosave system?

Destuol de interesant sa fiu sincer,e extrem de realist fata de viata reala.Am impuscat pe cine am vrut,am arestat pe oricine si am fac accidente si am dat vina pe altii,practic am fost un politai american.Misiunile sunt interesant si atatea optiuni dea aresta pe cineva,e extrem de fun si sper sa mai faca jocuri de genu
LOVE LOVE LOVE this janky simulator game! 😂 Me and my Daughter had sooooo much fun playing this on multiplayer!  Highly recommended bugs and all
Chaotic and fun! But if you play for too long, it can start to feel repetitive. I really enjoy the accident calls and the random encounters that lead to arrests
Glichy in multi player and ofc 2 player only  other than that good game.

I’m really enjoying the game overall. It has a lot of potential, and the core gameplay is fun, but there are a few areas that need improvement.

Needs polish:

- Vehicle driving physics feel rough and could be smoother.
- There are no parking tickets displayed on the front row of vehicles, making it hard to keep track of which vehicles have already been checked, especially in multiplayer.
- The GPS route seems to be based on which side of the road you are on, but it does not take the direction your vehicle is facing into account. This can cause it to switch back and forth between multiple routes.

Bugs/issues:

- Keyboard input feels slow or unresponsive when interacting with objects, and interactions sometimes do not trigger immediately.
- Police stations are way too bright after ending a shift.
- The game freezes for short periods of time.
- The GPS sometimes creates routes that go outside your district.
- Vehicles parked on the sidewalk sometimes do not count as illegal parking.
- Vehicles with a handicap sign can park anywhere and do not need a valid parking ticket.
- NPCs sometimes use the wrong gender when talking about other NPCs.
- There are invisible objects that can make it difficult to walk or drive.

I absolutely love this game! I was worried about it being repetitive as most games like this tend to be, but they have so many different things to do that I love. Sure some can get repetitive but I'm still unlocking new things, so I'm constantly adding new things to do. Definitely worth it in my opinion.

In a genre that sadly is very sparse for choices, Police Simulator: Patrol Officers stands out as the best police RP game at present.  This specifically focuses heavily on the concepts of probable cause, understanding offenses, fair justice and accountability.  By default, the game does punish you heavily for failures in these respects, but you can tailor the experience by adjusting the settings, allowing for variation between relaxed arcade and a more realistic simulation.  Of course it is not perfect, some scenarios can be a little clunky, but in fairness that is usually those that are more difficult to simulate with NPCs and not player controlled characters.  Content releases and feature expansions have been significant over the past several years, and most of the original bugs in the game have been fixed.  That said, probably the biggest thing the game needs at present is an optimization update, for while it does perform well with high settings, it does seem to pull more juice out of your PC than it should be, at least when comparing its graphics.  I suspect this is due to the lighting, which is impressive but can occasionally be a little oppressive.  However, I reiterate that that competitor games in genre are almost notoriously unfinished, badly coded and unrealistic failures that Police Sim currently leaves in the dust.  Police Sim is fair value for money that presents the best more realistic, singleplayer and coop police RP currently available; one with updates that continue to expand on the dynamism it previously lacked in earlier versions.  I like to think of this game as a proof of concept and test ground for a successor that built to support more advanced scenarios and mechanics based on the achievements of Police Sim.  Keep up the good work Aesir Interactive, and if your a gamer sick of crap police RPs or the insane requirements of LSPDFR variants, try out Police Sim!

I kept holding out hope for this game but the devs have clearly abandoned any attempt at fixing their game and instead just keep pumping out DLCs and skins while refusing to fix the absurd amount of glitches. And on top of that it is horribly optimized, with your character's hair seemingly taking up a huge quantity of the performance.
Played this for a while can be pretty boring aint that exciting unfortunately.

I was impressed with how in depth some of the content is.  For example the amount of investigating you can or choose not to do for the police reports I thought was very authentic.  Have not tried the expansion packs yet.

WHY IS THERE ONLY 2 PEOPLE IN CO-OP MODE? WHAT THE HELL IS THIS? I BOUGHT IT SPECIFICALLY TO PLAY WITH FRIENDS, BUT I CAN'T JOIN BECAUSE THE "SESSION IS OVERFLOWED." All normal games have a minimum of 4 players in co-op.

I pulled over a woman for driving with her phone in her right hand. She then showed me that she didn’t have a right hand. Of course I doubled down and gave her a ticket, BUT AT THE END OF THE DAY I WAS FIRED?! I didn’t even get a chance to not show up in court. Completely unrealistic
The unreal engine has made this unplayable random lags spikes.

First thing to remember about this game is that it feels and plays like an Early Access game, even though it isn't. This means there are bugs (including ones that will penalise you despite you doing things correctly) and there are a metric ton of graphical glitches that really should have been ironed out ages ago (such as NPCs passing through each other, vehicles rotating in a way physics never intended, and my personal favourite, your own character's leg keeps being stretched behind them).

That all said, it does its best to make an interesting and accountable police sim. If you want to drive round, smashing into walls and knocking pedestrians over, then that's what The Precinct is for. You hit a pedestrian in this game, then your shift ends immediately and you lose your points from that shift. You hit a bystander's car, even in pursuit, then you're expected to stop and deal with it.

The tutorials are a bit basic, and I found myself referring to Google a lot as to why I was getting things wrong. But, if you can handle the bugs and glitches, it's actually a great game underneath.
A surprisingly fun game to play with your mate. Has some bugs but can add to humor when playing. Plenty to do and genuinely enjoyable to play.

Police Simulator: Patrol Officers is a solid game if you enjoy slow, realistic, routine-based gameplay rather than constant action. The best part is how it makes small police tasks feel important, like checking IDs, issuing tickets, handling traffic stops, investigating accidents, and responding to calls.

The game is not perfect. Sometimes the AI acts weird, bugs can break immersion, and missions can feel repetitive after a while. But the core gameplay is satisfying, especially if you like roleplay and following procedures properly.

The open-world patrol system, vehicle patrols, and co-op mode make it more enjoyable. It is not a high-action police game, so do not expect GTA-style gameplay. This is more about patience, observation, and doing the job correctly.

Overall, I recommend it for players who like realistic simulation games and police roleplay. It still needs polish, but it is fun and worth trying, especially on sale.
fun game. a little glitchy at time but worth it
Great Relaxing Game with Cool Cars and Hilarious AI
